generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance DrivePositionEncoderLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder providing spatial measurement coverage for the left drive assembly of MABI MAX 100 robot with positional accuracy of 0.1mm and path repeatability of 0.06mm, integrated into motion control system for closed-loop validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DrivePositionDataLeft
]

instance DrivePositionEncoderRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder providing spatial measurement coverage for the right drive assembly of MABI MAX 100 robot with positional accuracy of 0.1mm and path repeatability of 0.06mm, integrated into motion control system for closed-loop validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DrivePositionDataRight
]

instance MotorTorqueSensorLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor integrated with left motor-gearbox assembly capturing real-time operational metrics for MABI MAX 100 robot, part of comprehensive sensing infrastructure for digital shadow monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTorqueDataLeft
]

instance MotorTorqueSensorRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor integrated with right motor-gearbox assembly capturing real-time operational metrics for MABI MAX 100 robot, part of comprehensive sensing infrastructure for digital shadow monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTorqueDataRight
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance DrivePositionDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DrivePositionEncoderLeft
]

instance DrivePositionDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DrivePositionEncoderRight
]

instance MotorTorqueDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorTorqueSensorLeft
]

instance MotorTorqueDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorTorqueSensorRight
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTraceAggregator : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Abstraction layer model implementing RFLP methodology to aggregate and correlate raw sensor data traces from physical system into meaningful contextual representations for digital shadow visualization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataIngestor
]

instance GearboxWearIndicatorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using SysML state machines to simulate and predict gearbox wear patterns based on aggregated torque and positional data traces, supporting predictive maintenance analytics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEngine
]

instance MotorDynamicBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation model implemented in MathWorks Simulink representing motor-gearbox interactions and kinematic behavior for real-time validation against physical system performance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEngine
]

instance AggregatedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data set containing correlated positional and torque measurements from all drive assemblies, serving as input for visualization and analytics services in the digital shadow system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DrivePositionDataLeft, DrivePositionDataRight, MotorTorqueDataLeft, MotorTorqueDataRight
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDataTraceAggregator
]

instance OperationalLogTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured operational log data containing timestamped system events and performance metrics derived from raw sensor streams, used for trend analysis and fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueDataLeft, MotorTorqueDataRight
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionAnalyzer
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataIngestor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data ingestion service implementing lightweight publish-subscribe protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital shadow components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowMonitoringService
]

instance SysMLVisualizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based visualization platform enabling dashboard-based representation of system states including motor torque, gearbox wear indicators, and positional feedback through internal block diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringDashboard
]

instance SimulinkExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink execution environment providing real-time simulation capabilities for dynamic behavior modeling and validation against physical system performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables GearboxWearIndicatorModel, MotorDynamicBehaviorModel
]

instance FaultDetectionAnalyzer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Purpose-specific analytics engine implementing rule-based fault detection algorithms on aggregated sensor data traces to identify potential gearbox wear or motor performance degradation."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

instance DataQualityValidator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data validation service ensuring quality and consistency of contextual data traces through cross-checking against model predictions and historical patterns before visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DigitalShadowMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive monitoring service exclusively providing real-time visualization of system states through aggregated contextual data traces without any bidirectional communication or control capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight, OperationalLogInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data aggregation service implementing abstraction layers to correlate raw sensor measurements into meaningful contextual representations for visualization and analytics purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ContextualDataInsight, AggregatedMetricsInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringDashboard : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard-based visualization service enabling stakeholders to observe motor torque, gearbox wear indicators, and positional feedback in real-time through SysML-based internal block diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Purpose-specific analytics service detecting potential faults in gearbox or motor components through analysis of aggregated torque and positional data traces."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, WearPredictionInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of current system states including motor torque levels, gearbox operational conditions, and positional feedback through dashboard interface."
]

instance OperationalLogInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured representation of operational logs containing timestamped system events and performance metrics for trend analysis and historical comparison."
]

instance ContextualDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual representation of sensor data traces providing correlated view of positional and torque measurements across all drive assemblies."
]

instance AggregatedMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Processed metrics derived from raw sensor data including average torque values, positional accuracy statistics, and operational efficiency indicators."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "SysML-based internal block diagram representation of system components and their current states enabling intuitive understanding of physical system conditions."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of potential faults in gearbox or motor components based on analysis of torque and positional data patterns against expected behavior models."
]

instance WearPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of gearbox wear patterns using state machine models to forecast potential degradation based on current operational conditions."
]

instance DataValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of data quality and consistency through cross-validation against model predictions and historical patterns to ensure reliable analytics."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical manufacturing environment containing MABI MAX 100 serial robot system with motor-gearbox-sensor assemblies operating under real-time monitoring requirements."
    base:contains DrivePositionEncoderLeft, DrivePositionEncoderRight, MotorTorqueSensorLeft, MotorTorqueSensorRight
]

// C5: Components
instance MABIMax100Robot : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Serial robot system (MABI MAX 100) with motor-gearbox-sensor assemblies operating in manufacturing automation domain, serving as physical counterpart to the digital shadow system."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance DigitalShadowSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Passive monitoring digital twin implementation providing real-time visualization of system states through aggregated contextual data traces without bidirectional communication capabilities."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
    base:contains ContextualDataTraceAggregator, GearboxWearIndicatorModel, MotorDynamicBehaviorModel, MQTTDataIngestor, SysMLVisualizationEngine, SimulinkExecutionEngine, FaultDetectionAnalyzer, DataQualityValidator
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents does not explicitly detail synchronization frequencies, time scales for services, latency requirements, or temporal granularity needs. However, the context implies that **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs like AADL, or MontiArc) are used to model system behavior, which inherently requires **temporal alignment** between physical and digital components. For example, **SysML state machines** or **MontiArc Automaton** may enforce discrete or continuous-time synchronization, though specific frequencies (e.g., millisecond-level updates) are not mentioned. The integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) suggests real-time or near-real-time synchronization for behavioral modeling, but precise temporal constraints (e.g., <100ms latency) are absent. **Not in Document** for explicit quantitative details, but the framework assumes **time-aware modeling** as a foundational requirement."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key **architecture patterns** include:
  - **Component & Connector Models**: ADLs like **ACME, AADL, or MontiArc** define systems as interconnected components with explicit interfaces, enabling modular twin deployment.
  - **Behavioral Integration**: **SysML state machines** or **MontiArc Automaton** provide behavioral coordination, while **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) or **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) handle detailed logic execution.
  - **Resource Management**: Implicitly addressed via **MBSE’s layered abstraction** (e.g., logical architecture isolating resource constraints), but no specific **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or **resource allocation policies** (e.g., QoS guarantees) are mentioned.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The document does not elaborate on **cross-twin synchronization** or **meta-coordination layers**, focusing instead on **individual twin modeling** and **data aggregation** (as in Digital Shadows). **Not in Document** for distributed orchestration strategies or fault-tolerance mechanisms."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Method)**, **SPES (System and Product Engineering System)**, and **SMArDT (Smart Digital Twin Methodology)**. Each layer employs distinct modeling notations:
- **Requirements layer**: Defines system objectives and constraints.
- **Functional architecture**: Describes system functions using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**.
- **Logical architecture**: Specifies components, interfaces, and connectors (e.g., **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines**).
- **Product/technical layer**: Integrates detailed physical models, simulations (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**), or code (e.g., **C++/Java**).

Lifecycle governance involves **transition management** between layers via **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior) and **stakeholder alignment** to ensure consistency across perspectives. The lifecycle is iterative, with **abstraction refinement** at each stage to bridge conceptual and executable representations. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are referenced in the document, but the process aligns with **MBSE best practices** for iterative refinement and validation.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system distinguishes itself from **passive Digital Shadows** by incorporating **active data processing components**, enabling bidirectional communication between the physical system and its digital counterpart. **Fidelity requirements** are implicitly addressed through **comparative analysis** of simulated vs. measured data, where deviations (e.g., **friction loss detection**) are used to refine model accuracy. **Uncertainty quantification** is inferred from **positional accuracy specifications (0.1 mm)** and **repeatability metrics (0.06 mm)**, which serve as benchmarks for validating model predictions. However, explicit **validation and verification (V&V) methodologies**, **error correction mechanisms**, or **formal quality assurance processes** are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems via standardized data streaming protocols**, specifically **MQTT**, for real-time sensor data acquisition and twin updates. instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-standard modeling languages and methodologies** to ensure interoperability and consistency, though explicit **certification frameworks** or **formal compliance requirements** are not detailed in the provided text. Key standards and specifications include:

1. **Modeling Languages**:
   - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** [17]: Used for **functional and logical architecture** modeling, including **Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** for component-connector decomposition and **state machines** (v1.7) for behavioral specification.
   - **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** [24]: Employed for **hardware-software co-design** and **real-time system analysis**, particularly in embedded or control-oriented DT applications.
   - **ACME (Architecture Description Language)** [23]: Leveraged for **generic architecture modeling**, though less prominently than SysML or AADL in this context.
   - **MontiArc** [25, 26]: A **model-driven engineering** tool for **automaton-based behavior modeling**, enabling formal specification of system dynamics.
   - **UML (Unified Modeling Language)**: Implied for **ontological or conceptual modeling** (e.g., digital shadow reference models in Fig. 1), though not explicitly named in the text.

2. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - **Ontological Alignment**: The text suggests **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML ↔ Simulink) via **shared ontologies** or **ontological mappings**, though no specific standard (e.g., **ISO 15926**, **OPC UA**) is cited.
   - **Data Exchange Formats**: While not explicitly mentioned, **XML-based schemas** (common in SysML/AADL) or **JSON** (for digital shadow data aggregation) are likely used for interoperability between tools.

3. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - No explicit mention of **industry certifications** (e.g., **ISO 26262** for automotive safety, **DO-333** for aviation) or **formal compliance requirements** (e.g., **SAE AS5506** for DTs). The text references **citation numbers** (e.g., [17], [24]) but lacks bibliographic details for specific standards.
   - **MBSE Methodologies**: Adherence to **structured engineering processes** (e.g., **RFLP** [18, 19], **OOSEM** [20]) ensures alignment with **ISO/IEC 19510** (MBSE guidelines), though this is inferred rather than explicitly stated.

4. **Lack of Explicit Details**:
   - No references to **formal verification standards** (e.g., **ISO 10218** for robotics), **security standards** (e.g., **NIST SP 800-53**), or **DT-specific frameworks** (e.g., **ISO/IEC 23247**).
   - **Interoperability protocols** (e.g., **OPC UA**, **ROS 2**) are not mentioned, despite their relevance to DT data exchange."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-relevant physical constraints** through the **safety cell** of the robotic arm (as referenced in [44]), which restricts the maximum working range to **2.25 meters** (as per the MABI Max-100 robotic arm specifications). This mechanism ensures **collision avoidance** and **operator safety** by defining a predefined operational envelope.

For **cybersecurity**, the system leverages **MQTT-based communication protocols** (as implied by [52], referencing Mosquitto server/client implementations) to facilitate secure data exchange between components. However, no explicit **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., encryption, role-based access control, or intrusion detection) are documented in the provided sources.

The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [47], [50]) includes **metadata descriptors** for assets, but these are not explicitly framed as security descriptors. The **RAMI 4.0 reference architecture ([49], [48])** emphasizes **interoperability and traceability** across automation layers, though it does not detail **safety-critical system considerations** beyond physical safeguards like the safety cell.

No references to **data anonymization**, **consent management**, or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001) are present in the documents. The **friction modeling** for robot dynamics ([45]) is described as improving **accuracy for high-precision applications**, not as a safety-critical measure.
"
]
